ApiCare Farmer Profile  ·  Jitlang Duga, East Sikkim

Dal Bhadur
Rai

Beekeeper & Master Trainer

Dal Bhadur began beekeeping in the forests of Jitlang Duga, East Sikkim — one of the most biodiverse valleys in the Eastern Himalayas. Today he is not only one of ApiCare's founding farmers but a certified trainer, teaching the next generation of beekeepers in the world's only 100% organic state.

🌿 Sikkim Certified Organic ✦ ApiCare Verified ⬡ Master Trainer

The ApiCare Farmer Network

Every jar of ApiCare honey is traceable to a verified farmer in Sikkim — the world's first and only 100% organic state, declared by the United Nations in 2018. We are building the trust infrastructure for India's organic economy.

125+
Farmers
5
Years
100%
Organic
🌎

Where This Honey Comes From

The Eastern Himalayas of Sikkim — a biodiversity hotspot with over 5,000 species of flowering plants. Bees forage across wild forests that have never seen a pesticide.

📱

How We Verify

Every harvest is recorded by the farmer via WhatsApp, verified by our ApiCare field team in Sikkim, and linked to this profile. No harvest enters our supply chain unverified.

💛

What Your Purchase Does

Product revenue flows directly to farmer payments. Dal Bhadur earns 3x the market rate because you can verify exactly who made your honey.
